journal_entry.description: Conversion of 50 preferred shares to 25 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: This is an equity conversion transaction. Under IFRS, when preferred shares are converted to common shares, we derecognize the preferred share equity account (debit to decrease equity) and recognize the common share equity account (credit to increase equity) at the carrying value of the preferred shares. The carrying value of $100 is transferred from Preferred Shares to Common Shares. No gain or loss is recognized on this equity-to-equity conversion. The tuples correctly identify this as an equity decrease (preferred) and equity increase (common).

output.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares involves reclassifying equity. The debit side decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) with carrying value $100. The credit side will increase Common Share Capital. This is an equity-to-equity reclassification with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income statement accounts.

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares involves reclassifying equity. The debit side decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) with carrying value $100. The credit side will increase Common Share Capital. This is an equity-to-equity reclassification with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income statement accounts.

output.reason: This is a share conversion transaction. On the credit side, we decrease Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) by crediting it. Under IFRS, crediting an equity account decreases its balance. The preferred shares are being removed/converted, so we credit Preferred Share Capital = equity decrease. However, equity decrease is not a credit category - it would be a debit. Let me reconsider: When converting preferred to common shares, we DEBIT preferred share capital (to decrease it) and CREDIT common share capital (to increase it). So on the CREDIT side, we are increasing Common Share Capital = equity increase (1 line).

credit_classifier.reason: This is a share conversion transaction. On the credit side, we decrease Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease) by crediting it. Under IFRS, crediting an equity account decreases its balance. The preferred shares are being removed/converted, so we credit Preferred Share Capital = equity decrease. However, equity decrease is not a credit category - it would be a debit. Let me reconsider: When converting preferred to common shares, we DEBIT preferred share capital (to decrease it) and CREDIT common share capital (to increase it). So on the CREDIT side, we are increasing Common Share Capital = equity increase (1 line).
